macro_summary: Friday's session extended the tech rout: SPY -0.53%, QQQ -1.20%, and SOXX cratered 5.03% as the AI capex sustainability debate intensified. The PCE inflation gauge hit a 3-year high at 4.1% headline / 3.4% core (per Thursday's Commerce Department release), reinforcing hawkish Fed expectations under Chair Warsh — though a Reuters poll out Friday showed over three-quarters of economists still expect rates to hold through year-end. WTI slipped to $70.24, now within a dollar of its pre-Hormuz level, as Strait of Hormuz shipping ramped up under the US-Iran memorandum. US10Y edged lower to 4.37% despite the hot inflation print, suggesting bond markets are reading the oil disinflation offset. MSFT bounced 4.94% off deeply oversold levels, but memory names sold off sharply — SNDK -9.76%, SK Hynix -8.36% on a 5.8% KOSPI tumble — even after Micron's blowout quarter midweek. Gold surged 2.1% to $4,103 in a safety bid.
commodities_note: WTI at $70.24 is essentially back to pre-conflict levels, down 20.8% over the month as Hormuz shipping normalizes — the oil risk premium has fully unwound. Gold's 2.1% jump to $4,103 on a day when yields fell and the dollar was flat suggests the bid is geopolitical hedging (Iran vessel strike Friday, per CBS News) rather than a rate trade. The 5-year breakeven at 2.21% shows inflation expectations are anchored despite the hot PCE print.

thesis_highlights: Blackwell Ultra ramp + Vera Rubin sampling are the multi-leg bull thesis — no confirmed revenue from Vera Rubin yet.

NVDA fiscal year offset from CY: Q4 FY2026 print = $68.1B (+73%), Q1 FY2027 guide = $78B (Feb 26 2026 earnings call). Vera Rubin samples shipping but contribute no booked revenue — Blackwell + Hopper still drive the print.
NVDA supply gated by TSMC CoWoS allocation and SK Hynix HBM3E — supply is the near-term production ceiling.

writeup.headline: RSI reset to 37.6, hugging the 200-day — bull case needs volume confirmation.
writeup.what_to_do: NVDA pulled back another 1.2% Friday to $192.53, now 8.3% below its 50-day with RSI at 37.6 — near oversold. The 200-day SMA at $190.43 is just 1.1% below, creating a razor-thin headline stop that distorts the R:R (headline 2.3:1 to $197.39 resistance, but sizing R:R to structural support at $177.20 is only 0.3:1). Blackwell Ultra is ramping and Vera Rubin moved to full production (per NVDA newsroom, Jun 2026), keeping the multi-leg thesis intact. Wait for a volume-backed reclaim of the 200-day and a move above $194.44 (nearest resistance) before upgrading to BUY. Pre-earnings clarity: no earnings within 14 days — the setup is technical, not binary.
writeup.tight_upside_disclosure: Nearest resistance at $194.44 (1.0% above) — headline R:R only materialises once that level breaks on above-average volume. Any upgrade to BUY is conditional on $194.44 breaking on above-average volume.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if NVDA fails to reclaim SMA50 ($209.92) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

The MI450 framework deal with Meta (per AMD IR, Feb 2026) is a ceiling, not firm purchase orders — revenue is contingent on MI450 benchmarks, and no new customer wins have been announced since. Wait for a consolidation toward the 50-day at $439, or a confirmed second hyperscaler MI450 win to add a second thesis leg. thesis_highlights: HBM3E supply tight through 2026-27 — AI training demand structurally separated from inference-efficiency risk.

writeup.headline: +41% extreme extension — Micron's blowout quarter can't override the entry math.
writeup.what_to_do: MU posted Q3 EPS of $25.11 (+21% beat, per MU IR Jun 25) and the stock still fell 4.7% Friday — the earnings-driven extension had already priced the beat. At +41% above the 50-day, this is extreme extension territory where no trend exception applies. The structural conviction is high: EPS accelerated 4.78 → 12.2 → 25.11 over three quarters, HBM3E is supply-constrained through 2027, and forward PE of 7.6x (74% discount to the semis cluster) is the cheapest in the group. But the SMA50 is 29% below price — a pullback to it would require a crash. Use the $854 support zone as the realistic re-entry target. The thesis is intact; the price is simply too far from any sensible entry. Revisit on a 20-25% pullback toward $854-900.

EPS trajectory is accelerating (17,850 → 21,522 → 56,670 KRW over three quarters), and forward PE of 6.2x is the cheapest in the semis cluster. But the SMA50 is 28% below — no sensible entry exists. Use the KRW 1,855,000 support zone as the realistic re-entry target. Fragility: Samsung HBM3E qualification progress is the bear case — any yield improvement would break the duopoly.

writeup.headline: Downgraded to CAUTION — -18.1% week, R:R distorted, FCF deeply negative.

The Cantor Fitzgerald $167 PT (per Insider Monkey, Jun 26) offers no floor — the bond-offering disclosure cited by the analyst hasn't stabilised the tape. Headline R:R of 2.4:1 is distorted by a 1.8% stop to $94.82; sizing R:R to structural support at $91.02 is 0.7:1. FCF yield of -16.25% means the GPU fleet is consuming cash, not generating it — the neocloud model depends on utilisation staying high as hyperscalers build their own capacity (per the standing thesis guardrails). Revisit on a reclaim of the 50-day with volume, or after the next hyperscaler capex update confirms third-party GPU demand is holding.

Forward PE of 190x prices in WSE-3 dominance that the S-1 itself frames with OpenAI warrant distortion (per the standing IA thesis — hardware gross margin is 43% and the $20B OpenAI deal value overstates true economics). The architectural ceilings on I/O bandwidth and FP8 support (per IA engineering analysis) are real technical limits. No entry until the stock builds a multi-month trading range with observable support. Wait.

writeup.headline: -42.9% in a month — InP thesis still real, but China export permits gate revenue.
writeup.what_to_do: AXTI's AI-InP narrative took a -17.1% hit this week alone, now 28.1% below its 50-day at $70.15. The Q1 backlog of ~$100M and guided Q2 profitability (per AXTI IR, Q1 2026 call) are real, but China's InP export-control list means every overseas shipment needs a government permit — revenue conversion is gated on opaque, timing-volatile approvals. Forward PE of 90x fails the valuation gate, and R:R of 0.3:1 to $71.49 resistance is broken. AXTI needs permit-flow evidence — a quarter with shipments matching backlog — before the thesis can be traded with conviction. Revisit after Q2 earnings (34 days) if the permit environment shows sustained throughput.

The €41M SmartSiC write-down in H1 FY26 and -34.6% revenue decline mean the legacy SiC/RF-SOI base is contracting faster than the photonics-SOI AI thesis can grow. At €114.35, the stock is 17.3% below its 50-day with RSI at 41.5 — no technical support is forming. Forward PE of 58x prices in a photonics ramp that is early-stage and unproven at scale. R:R of 0.5:1 to €138.20 resistance with a €62.50 invalidation (200-day SMA) is unfavourable. Revisit when photonics-SOI revenue appears as a separate line item in earnings, confirming the AI-optical demand is material relative to the legacy base.

writeup.headline: +37% extended, -9.8% Friday — NAND cycle peak fears hit the tape.
writeup.what_to_do: SNDK cratered 9.8% Friday to $2,090.71 as the NAND ASP cycle collided with broad semis selling. The stock is still 37.4% above its 50-day — extreme extension territory. Forward PE of 11.4x looks cheap, but the trailing-to-forward PE collapse (71.6 → 11.4) is the tell: the market is pricing a cyclical earnings peak, not a structural re-rate. PEG of 0.02 is anomalous — verify the earnings growth estimate; a 251% revenue growth rate is unlikely to sustain. NAND is more commoditised and consumer-cyclical than HBM — SanDisk has no HBM exposure, so the MU/SK Hynix training-demand arguments don't transfer. The structural conviction is 'emerging' (thesis unconfirmed). Revisit on a pullback toward the $1,514 support zone for a better R:R.

writeup.headline: +4.9% bounce off deeply oversold — R:R 2.2:1, but non-OpenAI RPO is the clean read.
writeup.what_to_do: MSFT snapped its losing streak with a 4.9% bounce to $372.97 after RSI hit 28.8 — the most oversold reading in over a year. R:R is 2.2:1 to $412.16 resistance from $355.51 support — favourable, and all eight ACCUMULATE mechanical gates passed. But the ACCUMULATE upgrade is withheld: (1) no structural support cite above $355.51 exists (200-day SMA at $446 is far above, not below), (2) ~45% of the $625B commercial RPO is OpenAI-linked, making Azure AI revenue quality the unresolved thesis risk. Non-OpenAI commercial RPO (+28%) is the cleaner demand signal (per MSFT IR). Stifel cut its PT to $400 on AI margin concerns (per Yahoo Finance, Jun 26). Watch for a volume-backed push above $412.16 — that would confirm Friday's bounce as more than tactical relief. Earnings July 30 (33 days).

The headline R:R of 8.3:1 is distorted by a 1.6% stop, but sizing R:R to structural support at $778.24 is 2.8:1 — the most favourable risk-adjusted setup in the semis cluster. EPS is accelerating (1.10 → 1.67 → 2.37, per LITE IR), and the NVDA $2B photonics investment anchors the multi-year thesis. Forward PE of 45x fails the entry block — the 90% revenue growth partially justifies it, but the multiple demands flawless execution. Greensboro fab buildout is the structural moat (per IA May 2026). Wait for a test of the $778-804 support zone with volume before entry; the R:R at those levels would be compelling. Fragility: single-catalyst thesis — NVDA photonics partnership is the only confirmed hyperscaler commitment.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 45x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if multiple compresses toward the Semis cluster median (29.6x) without a corresponding earnings re-rating.

writeup.headline: Downgraded to CAUTION — -6.9% today collapses R:R from 2.5:1 to 0.7:1.
writeup.what_to_do: NOK's ACCUMULATE signal from yesterday is invalidated: the stock dropped 6.9% Friday to $13.01, breaking below the prior entry zone and compressing R:R from 2.5:1 to 0.7:1. The 50-day SMA was lost intraday — price is now 4.0% below it with RSI at 43.0. The NVDA Spectrum-X and 800G/1.6T optical thesis is intact, but the entry math no longer works. Forward PE of 26.7x and FCF yield of 2.2% pass the on_deck fundamental gates, but the technical damage is fresh. Wait for a reclaim of the 50-day at $13.55 with above-average volume — that's the minimum bar to revisit. Earnings July 23 (26 days) is the next catalyst. Nokia's slow-cycle compounder profile means patience is warranted.

BE.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 58x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

rcp_state.terms_summary: Oracle 'Project Jupiter' deal — the structural AI-power catalyst behind the +10.7% gap (per 2026-06-04 report). Natural-gas dependency + dilution history are the standing risks.

writeup.headline: RCP consolidation: Session 16/25 — Oracle Project Jupiter catalyst, no entry yet.
writeup.what_to_do: BE crashed 18.3% Friday to $252.02 — the worst single-day drop on the watchlist — as AI power hype cooled on competing gas-turbine and nuclear options (per Simply Wall St., Jun 26). The RCP state machine is in consolidation phase: session 16 of the 25-session gate following the Oracle Project Jupiter deal (signed contract). Graduation triggers: Path A — SMA50 retest near $273.70 with volume hold; Path B — breakout above the 8% consolidation band ceiling. Failure triggers: 5+ closes below $273.70 (post-gap SMA50) AND below $272.91 (95% of gap settle) within 60 sessions; or 3+ closes below the pre-gap close of $302.85. The natural-gas dependency and dilution history are the standing bear-case risks. No entry until graduation — wait for the 25-session gate to clear (9 more sessions) and a confirmed Path A or B trigger.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 58x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

IFX_DE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +13.5% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: 13.5% (>=10% ceiling for trend exception).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

writeup.headline: +13.5% extended — the SiC datacenter pivot thesis is real, the entry is not.
writeup.what_to_do: IFX pulled back 4.5% Friday to €78.30 but remains 13.5% above its 50-day — past the 10% trend-exception ceiling. The IA thesis (May 2026) on Infineon's 1200V SiC dominance pivoting from auto to AI datacenter power infrastructure is directionally intact, but the auto/EV exposure is the bear-case anchor — Chinese SiC price competition (BYD, Wolfspeed) hasn't resolved. PEG of 0.76 and forward PE of 30.0x pass the on_deck gate, and FCF yield of 1.33% is borderline. R:R of 1.1:1 to €88.46 resistance is unfavourable. Wait for a consolidation toward the 50-day at €68.97 or a named hyperscaler SiC power-supply contract win to add a second thesis leg.
writeup.entry_block: BLOCKED: +13.5% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: 13.5% (>=10% ceiling for trend exception).

AIXA_DE.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 40x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

writeup.headline: Upgraded to HOLD — at the SMA50, RSI neutral, GaN MOCVD monopoly thesis intact.
writeup.what_to_do: AIXA is consolidating right at its 50-day SMA (€52.12 vs €52.23), with RSI at 45.4 — the -13.3% weekly pullback has reset the technicals to neutral. The GaN MOCVD tool monopoly thesis (per IA May 2026) is a multi-year capital-equipment cycle story, not a momentum trade. R:R of 2.8:1 to €55.28 resistance with a €50.98 invalidation is favourable, but forward PE of 40x and PEG of 2.08 flag the valuation as stretched for a name with -47% revenue growth. Wide-stop R:R to €45.72 structural support is only 0.5:1 — the headline R:R borrows optimism from the tight stop. Revisit on a volume-backed bounce off the 50-day; a break below €50.98 would downgrade to CAUTION.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 40x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

writeup.headline: Downgraded to CAUTION — -28% in a week, GaN thesis buried under the tape.
writeup.what_to_do: NVTS cratered 28% this week to $17.30, now 17.9% below its 50-day. The GaN power-semiconductor thesis has no near-term catalyst to arrest the slide — no recent news, no named design wins, and the broad semis selloff is indiscriminately punishing small-cap names. R:R of 0.2:1 is broken. RSI at 38.7 is approaching oversold but there's no support to lean on until the $14.95 zone — another 13.6% below. Wait for the stock to form a base at $14.95-15.00 and for a catalyst — a named hyperscaler GaN power-supply win or a datacenter partnership — before revisiting. The on_deck fundamentals are unavailable (no PE/PEG data), so patience is the only edge.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 54x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

writeup.headline: Downgraded to CAUTION — RSI 34.5 is contrarian territory, but 54x PE gates it.
writeup.what_to_do: PLTR snapped a 7-day losing streak with a 4.7% bounce to $112.93, but RSI at 34.5 and a -17.2% gap below the 50-day keep it in the penalty box. The defense AI spending surge (Army $10B IDIQ framework, Navy $448M ShipOS — per DoD press releases) is a real catalyst, but IDIQ ceilings are not firm revenue. EPS is accelerating (0.21 → 0.25 → 0.33), and R:R of 2.0:1 to $136.10 resistance is at the WATCH threshold. But forward PE of 54.2x (133% premium to the BigTech cluster) prices in perfection, and FCF yield of 0.65% fails the on_deck cash-generation gate. Wait for a volume-backed reclaim of the $136.10 resistance — that level would confirm the bounce as more than a dead-cat. Fragility: single-catalyst thesis — defense AI spending is the only confirmed growth driver.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 54x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if multiple compresses toward the BigTech cluster median (23.3x) without a corresponding earnings re-rating.

thesis_highlights: ADR/China delisting risk is a standing consideration — always maintain starter sizing for WRD.

writeup.headline: Downgraded to CAUTION — RSI 30.2 deeply oversold, but ADR delisting risk caps conviction.
writeup.what_to_do: WRD is the most oversold name on the watchlist at RSI 30.2, down 24.6% over the month. At $5.50, it's 23.4% below its declining 50-day — no technical support is forming. The L4 autonomy thesis (Uber, Grab, Bosch partnerships, 8 countries) is commercially unproven at ~$98M revenue, and the ADR/China delisting risk is a standing thesis invalidator. R:R of 2.1:1 to $6.68 resistance is borderline — the headline ratio looks favourable only because the downside to $4.95 support is 10%, which is wide for a pre-profit ADR. Hold no action: WRD needs a catalyst — a confirmed fleet-scale deployment with named revenue or a delisting resolution — before the thesis is tradable. Revisit after Q2 earnings (33 days) for commercial traction evidence.

SITM.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 62x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

writeup.headline: Upgraded to HOLD — at the SMA50 after a -7.8% week, MEMS timing thesis intact.
writeup.what_to_do: SITM settled at $672.19, essentially flat against its 50-day SMA ($672.64) after a 7.8% weekly pullback. RSI at 47.2 is perfectly neutral — the extreme extension from a month ago has fully reset. The MEMS precision timing displacement thesis (per IA May 2026) is a multi-year quartz-to-MEMS transition story, and eight straight quarters of triple-digit profit growth (per IBD, Jun 25) confirm the trajectory. But the headline R:R of 6.6:1 is distorted — wide-stop R:R to $609.15 structural support is 1.54:1, which clears the gate but just barely. Forward PE of 62x and PEG of 3.82 fail the on_deck valuation gate. Wait for a volume-backed bounce off the 50-day and a break above $706 (the SMA10) to upgrade to WATCH. The thesis is real; the entry needs confirmation.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 62x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

singapore.summary: DBS and OCBC upgraded to WATCH as the pipeline applied the sustained-trend exception (60+ sessions above rising 50-day, RSI under 65). UOB remains the outlier — hard-blocked on RSI 66.7. The cluster's NIM-compression headwind is unchanged: US10Y at 4.37% is still above the 4.20% relief threshold for the USD-funding-cost channel, and the next MAS review isn't until October. OCBC's relative outperformance (YoY profit growth while DBS/UOB declined QoQ) remains the intra-cluster differentiator.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally compressed, tactically trending — NIM relief is a macro call, not a stock call.
singapore.key_development: DBS and OCBC received trend exceptions from the pipeline, upgrading them to WATCH despite >7% extension — the first signal improvement in the Singapore cluster in weeks.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX cratered 5.03% Friday, capping a -7.74% week — the sharpest weekly decline since the March rout. Eight of twelve semis names are hard-blocked on extension, and the two that aren't (NVDA, TSEM) are below their 50-day averages. The cluster is now dominated by downside momentum: memory names (MU, SK Hynix, SNDK) sold off despite Micron's blowout quarter, signaling that earnings beats are already priced. The AI capex sustainability debate is the sector-level headwind — $850B in hyperscaler spending (per NY Post) with no confirmed ROI is the weight on every semis ticker. NVDA's RSI reset to 37.6 and AVGO's approach to the 200-day are the setups to watch if the selling exhausts.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically broken — AI demand is real, but the price extension priced in flawless execution that the tape is now correcting.
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX -7.74% week despite MU's Q3 blowout ($25.11 EPS, +21% beat) — the market is no longer rewarding semis beats, a regime-shift signal for the cluster.

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster is diverging on the technicals but converging on the narrative: all three names are now grappling with AI ROI questions. MSFT bounced 4.94% off deeply oversold RSI (28.8) but the Stifel PT cut to $400 on AI margin concerns (per Yahoo Finance, Jun 26) and the OpenAI credit-circularity risk keep it on WATCH. GOOG continued to slide (-2.1%) as AI talent exits (two high-profile departures in two weeks, per Motley Fool) compound the TPU ramp story. AMZN is testing its 200-day SMA — a breach would be the first since early 2025. PLTR's 4.7% bounce snapped a 7-day losing streak but the 54x PE and IDIQ-vs-firm-revenue distinction keep it in the CAUTION column.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, AI customer demand unproven; MSFT's Azure ex-OpenAI RPO and GOOG's TPU adoption are the metrics that cut through the noise.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's 4.94% bounce was the first green shoot in weeks, but the Stifel downgrade on AI margin pressure shows Wall Street is now pricing the cost side of the AI buildout, not just the revenue opportunity.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA's parallel $2B photonics investment alongside LITE — S&P 500 member with direct AI datacenter optical-interconnect revenue exposure.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 15-20% from current levels on a broad semis selloff — the NVDA photonics anchor is structural, not cyclical.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC competitor to AVGO, AI networking and XPU wins — the AVGO-MRVL divergence on gross margins (per 24/7 Wall St., Jun 26) makes MRVL the higher-beta custom-silicon play.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AVGO earnings or the next hyperscaler custom-silicon roadmap update — MRVL's 231% YTD gains imply it will correct harder than AVGO on any custom-ASIC demand scare.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: HBM3E competitor to SK Hynix — Samsung's qualification struggles have tightened the HBM duopoly; any yield improvement news would break the MU/SK Hynix supply-constraint thesis.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung announces HBM3E qualification at a named hyperscaler — that would be the structural bear signal for MU and 000660.KS margins.

interconnected.name: Qualcomm
interconnected.reason: Snapdragon AI edge chips leverage Arm architecture — surfaces as an on-device AI inference play as the AI capex debate shifts from training to inference at the edge.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if on-device AI adoption accelerates (AAPL or MSFT Copilot+ PC sales data confirms) — QCOM is the proxy for AI inference moving off the datacenter GPU.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter switching leader — 800G/1.6T optical deployments are the networking layer beneath NVDA GPU clusters and LITE/COHR photonics.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if hyperscaler capex commentary confirms 800G/1.6T switch deployments accelerating — ANET is the pick-and-shovel on the networking side of the AI buildout.

active_risks: Hormuz de-escalation continues — WTI at $70.24 is within $1 of pre-conflict levels as Strait of Hormuz shipping ramps under the US-Iran memorandum, but Iran's Friday strike on a commercial vessel (per CBS News / Ynetnews) signals the agreement is fragile.

US-China tech decoupling escalates — China added 10 US entities (MP Materials, USA Rare Earth, defense firms) to its export control list with immediate prohibition (per Benchmark Mineral Intelligence, Jun 25); Alibaba sued the US over its defense blacklist.
Trump threatens 100% tariffs on any country imposing digital services taxes on US tech firms, singling out European nations — would override existing trade agreements (per Ynetnews, Jun 26).

Fed hawkish repricing — PCE hit 3-year high at 4.1% headline / 3.4% core, reinforcing rate-hike expectations under Chair Warsh, though a Reuters poll shows 75% of economists still expect a hold through year-end.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil's return to pre-war levels removes the energy-premium headwind that had weighed on rate-sensitive tech for months — but the PCE inflation surge is now the dominant macro force keeping semis and BigTech under pressure. The SOXX -7.74% week reflects both the hawkish rate backdrop and the AI capex ROI debate; MSFT's bounce and gold's 2.1% surge suggest some capital is rotating to safety and oversold mega-caps. Singapore banks remain anchored to the US10Y — until the 4.20% threshold breaks, NIM relief is not priced.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran struck a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz just hours after the IRGC warned ships not to use unapproved shipping lanes (per Ynetnews / CBS News, Jun 26) — the first violation of the US-Iran memorandum of understanding. Oil didn't spike on the news ($70.24, -0.5%), suggesting the market views it as a tactical provocation rather than a re-closure signal.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds at de-escalated levels (WTI $65-75), PCE inflation stays sticky near 3.5-4% keeping the Fed on hold through year-end, and US-China tech decoupling remains at current tit-for-tat intensity without broadening to semiconductor capital equipment. Semis cluster consolidates below 50-day averages with NVDA and AVGO testing structural support; BigTech grinds sideways on AI ROI doubts.

optimistic.description: US-Iran peace deal formalised, WTI drops below $65 fully unwinding the risk premium, and the July NFP print shows labor market cooling — the Fed signals a cut by September. Tech multiples re-rate higher; semis cluster reclaims 50-day averages led by NVDA and TSM; MSFT rallies through $412 as AI demand fears ease.

pessimistic.description: Iran vessel strike escalates into a broader Hormuz disruption, WTI spikes back above $90, and the July 2 NFP prints hot — BofA's 75bps rate-hike call materialises. Semis cluster breaks below 200-day averages; GOOG and AMZN breach structural support; only memory names (MU, SK Hynix) hold on HBM supply constraints.

wildcard.description: Trump's 100% digital-tax tariff threat triggers a EU-US trade war within weeks — European Commission retaliates on US tech exports, directly hitting ASML, IFX, and AIXA. EUR-denominated names sell off sharply; the MATCH Act accelerates through Congress as a bargaining chip. ASML drops below $1,440 support on dual US-EU regulatory overhang.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Deploy no new capital — 8 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension and the semis cluster is in a regime-shift correction. Wait for the July 2 NFP print to resolve the rate-hike vs hold debate before committing to any entry, and use the pullback in NVDA and AVGO toward structural support as the watch-level for the next actionable setup.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — semis correction deepening, cash preservation favoured
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 8 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension. The semis cluster (SOXX -7.74% week) is in its sharpest correction since March, and the PCE inflation surge at 4.1% reinforces the hawkish Fed backdrop that punishes duration-sensitive AI capex names. Singapore banks are the relative bright spot with two WATCH upgrades, but no ticker carries a BUY or ACCUMULATE signal — the portfolio is in a holding pattern until the rate picture clears.

NAND cycle play, forward PE 11.4x. PEG anomalous — verify earnings growth. Extreme extension blocks entry. Revisit on pullback to $1,514 support zone.

caution_minus_hold.reason: CAUTION, HOLD not readable (thin-n or single-regime)

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=3) / ACCUMULATE (n=4) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
